turns.user_message: Someone just handed me another heavy pile of expectations and told me I always handle things so smoothly. I smiled and nodded like usual, but the words actually felt like a heavy weight settling on my chest. This whole reaction just started recently, and I cannot quite shake it off. I keep staring at the same empty space, trying to muster up the usual energy, but it simply is not there. I feel completely drained before I even touch the first item on the list.

They keep handing me more things and expecting that same effortless energy I used to fake so easily. Every single comment about how reliable I am now just echoes in my head like a broken record. I have been dragging myself through these days for a couple of weeks now, pretending everything is completely fine. My focus keeps slipping away, and I catch myself staring blankly at the same routine over and over. I just feel hollowed out, completely disconnected from anything I used to actually care about.

Someone just tossed another impossible request my way with that same cheerful remark about how I never let them down. I physically froze in my seat while my hands started trembling over nothing at all. I have been running on empty for so long that my entire mind and body feel completely fried. I sit here watching the hours slip by while my mind just refuses to process a single thought. I am utterly paralyzed by the sheer weight of it all, and I honestly do not know how to keep pretending.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This just started recently.
